name: HoleyFocusList

unions.name: HoleyFocusList
unions.comment:
Represents a special kind of list with items of type `a`.

The type `focus` can be

  - `đźŤŤ đźŤ“ <đźŤŠ> đźŤ‰ đźŤ‡`: use [`Item`](#Item) to require such an argument

  - `đźŤŤ đźŤ“ <?> đźŤ‰ đźŤ‡`: use a type variable to require such an argument

    `<?>` means both are possible:

      - `đźŤŤ đźŤ“ <> đźŤ‰ đźŤ‡`: a hole between items ... Heh.
      - `đźŤŤ đźŤ“ <đźŤŠ> đźŤ‰ đźŤ‡`

aliases.name: Item
aliases.comment:
A `HoleyFocusList Item a` is focussed on an element of type `a`.

```monospace
đźŤŤ đźŤ“ <đźŤŠ> đźŤ‰ đźŤ‡
```

values.name: after
values.comment:
The items after the current focussed location in the `HoleyFocusList`.

```monospace
đźŤŤ đźŤ“ <đźŤŠ> (đźŤ‰ đźŤ‡
```

    HoleyFocusList.only 0
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ 1, 2, 3 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.next
        |> Maybe.map HoleyFocusList.after
    --> Just [ 2, 3 ]
values.type: HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus_ a -> List.List a

values.name: afterLast
values.comment:
Focus the hole after the end of the `HoleyFocusList`. Into the nothingness.

```monospace
đźŤ“ <đźŤŠ> đźŤ‰  ->  đźŤ“ đźŤŠ đźŤ‰ <>
```

    import ListIs

    HoleyFocusList.only 1                 -- <1>
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ 2, 3 ] -- <1> 2 3
        |> HoleyFocusList.afterLast       -- 1 2 3 <>
        |> HoleyFocusList.plug 4          -- 1 2 3 <4>
        |> HoleyFocusList.joinParts
    --> ListIs.fromCons 1 [ 2, 3, 4 ]
values.type: HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus_ a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList (MaybeIs.CanBe hole_ ()) a

values.name: append
values.comment:
Put items to the end of the `HoleyFocusList`. After anything else.

```monospace
              đźŤ’đźŤ‹
đźŤ“ <đźŤŠ> đźŤ‰ đźŤ‡ â†“/
```

    import ListIs

    HoleyFocusList.only 123
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ 456 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ 789, 0 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.joinParts
    --> ListIs.fromCons 123 [ 456, 789, 0 ]
values.type: List.List a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus a

values.name: before
values.comment:
The items before the location of the focus in the `HoleyFocusList`.

```monospace
đźŤŤ đźŤ“) <đźŤŠ> đźŤ‰ đźŤ‡
```

    HoleyFocusList.only 0
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ 1, 2, 3 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.next
        |> Maybe.andThen HoleyFocusList.next
        |> Maybe.map HoleyFocusList.before
    --> Just [ 0, 1 ]
values.type: HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus_ a -> List.List a

values.name: beforeFirst
values.comment:
Focus the hole before the first item.
Remember that holes surround everything!

```monospace
đźŤŤ đźŤ“ <đźŤŠ> đźŤ‰  ->  <> đźŤŤ đźŤ“ đźŤŠ đźŤ‰
```

    import ListIs

    HoleyFocusList.only 1                 -- <1>
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ 3, 4 ] -- <1> 3 4
        |> HoleyFocusList.nextHole        -- 1 <> 3 4
        |> HoleyFocusList.plug 2          -- 1 <2> 3 4
        |> HoleyFocusList.beforeFirst     -- <> 1 2 3 4
        |> HoleyFocusList.plug 0          -- <0> 1 2 3 4
        |> HoleyFocusList.joinParts
    --> ListIs.fromCons 0 [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ]
values.type: HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus_ a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList (MaybeIs.CanBe hole_ ()) a

values.name: branchableType
values.comment:
When using a `HoleyFocusList Item ...` argument,
its type can't be unified with non-`Item` lists.

Please read more at [`MaybeIs.branchableType`](MaybeIs#branchableType).
values.type: HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList HoleyFocusList.Item a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList item_ a

values.name: current
values.comment:
The current focussed item in the `HoleyFocusList`.

```monospace
đźŤŤ đźŤ“ <đźŤŠ> đźŤ‰ đźŤ‡  ->  đźŤŠ
```

    HoleyFocusList.only "hi there"
        |> HoleyFocusList.current
    --> "hi there"

    HoleyFocusList.only 1
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ 2, 3, 4 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.last
        |> HoleyFocusList.current
    --> 4
values.type: HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList HoleyFocusList.Item a -> a

values.name: empty
values.comment:
An empty `HoleyFocusList` focussed on a hole with nothing before
and after it.
It's the loneliest of all `HoleyFocusList`s.

```monospace
<>
```

    import ListIs

    HoleyFocusList.empty
        |> HoleyFocusList.joinParts
    --> ListIs.empty
values.type: HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList (MaybeIs.CanBe hole_ ()) a_

values.name: findBackward
values.comment:
Find the first item in the `HoleyFocusList` matching a predicate, moving backwards
from the current position.

    HoleyFocusList.only 4
        |> HoleyFocusList.prepend [ 2, -1, 0, 3 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.findBackward (\item -> item < 0)
        |> Maybe.map HoleyFocusList.current
    --> Just -1
values.type: (a -> Basics.Bool) -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus_ a -> Maybe.Maybe (HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList item_ a)

values.name: findForward
values.comment:
Find the first item in the `HoleyFocusList` the matches a predicate, returning a
`HoleyFocusList` focussed on that item if it was found.

This start from the current focussed location and searches towards the end.

    HoleyFocusList.only 4
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ 2, -1, 0, 3 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.findForward (\item -> item < 0)
        |> Maybe.map HoleyFocusList.current
    --> Just -1

    HoleyFocusList.only -4
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ 2, -1, 0, 3 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.findForward (\item -> item < 0)
        |> Maybe.map HoleyFocusList.current
    --> Just -4
values.type: (a -> Basics.Bool) -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus_ a -> Maybe.Maybe (HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList item_ a)

values.name: first
values.comment:
Focus the first item in the `HoleyFocusList`.

```monospace
đźŤŤ đźŤ“ <đźŤŠ> đźŤ‰  ->  <đźŤŤ> đźŤ“ đźŤŠ đźŤ‰
```

    HoleyFocusList.only 1
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ 2, 3, 4 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.prepend [ 4, 3, 2 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.first
        |> HoleyFocusList.current
    --> 4
values.type: HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus a

values.name: focussesItem
values.comment:
Find out if the current focussed thing is an item.

    import MaybeIs

    HoleyFocusList.only 3
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ 2, 1 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.nextHole
        |> HoleyFocusList.focussesItem
    --> MaybeIs.nothing
values.type: HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList (MaybeIs.CanBe holeTag_ yesOrNever) a -> MaybeIs.MaybeIs (MaybeIs.CanBe emptyTag_ yesOrNever) (HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList item_ a)

values.name: insertAfter
values.comment:
Insert an item after the focussed location.

```monospace
        đźŤ’
đźŤ“ <đźŤŠ> â†“ đźŤ‰ đźŤ‡
```

    import ListIs

    HoleyFocusList.only 123
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ 789 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.insertAfter 456
        |> HoleyFocusList.joinParts
    --> ListIs.fromCons 123 [ 456, 789 ]

Insert multiple items using [`squeezeInAfter`](#squeezeInAfter).
values.type: a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus a

values.name: insertBefore
values.comment:
Insert an item before the focussed location.

```monospace
      đźŤ’
đźŤŤ đźŤ“ â†“ <đźŤŠ> đźŤ‰
```

    import ListIs

    HoleyFocusList.only 123
        |> HoleyFocusList.insertBefore 456
        |> HoleyFocusList.joinParts
    --> ListIs.fromCons 456 [ 123 ]

Insert multiple items using [`squeezeInBefore`](#squeezeInBefore).
values.type: a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus a

values.name: joinParts
values.comment:
Flattens the `HoleyFocusList` into a [`ListIs`](ListIs):

    import ListIs

    HoleyFocusList.empty
        |> HoleyFocusList.joinParts
    --> ListIs.empty

    HoleyFocusList.only 123
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ 789 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.nextHole
        |> HoleyFocusList.plug 456
        |> HoleyFocusList.joinParts
    --> ListIs.fromCons 123 [ 456, 789 ]

the type information gets carried over, so

    Item -> ListIs.NotEmpty
    CanBe hole_ () -> CanBe empty_ ()
values.type: HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList (MaybeIs.CanBe holeTag_ yesOrNever) a -> ListIs.ListIs (MaybeIs.CanBe emptyTag_ yesOrNever) a

values.name: last
values.comment:
Focus the last item in the `HoleyFocusList`.

```monospace
đźŤ“ <đźŤŠ> đźŤ‰ đźŤ‡  ->  đźŤ“ đźŤŠ đźŤ‰ <đźŤ‡>
```

    HoleyFocusList.only 1
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ 2, 3, 4 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.last
        |> HoleyFocusList.current
    --> 4

    HoleyFocusList.only 1
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ 2, 3, 4 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.last
        |> HoleyFocusList.before
    --> [ 1, 2, 3 ]
values.type: HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus a

values.name: map
values.comment:
Execute a function on every item in the `HoleyFocusList`.

    import ListIs

    HoleyFocusList.only "first"
        |> HoleyFocusList.prepend [ "zeroth" ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ "second", "third" ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.map String.toUpper
        |> HoleyFocusList.joinParts
    --> ListIs.fromCons "ZEROTH" [ "FIRST", "SECOND", "THIRD" ]
values.type: (a -> b) -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList (MaybeIs.CanBe hole_ yesOrNever) a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList (MaybeIs.CanBe mappedHole_ yesOrNever) b

values.name: mapAfter
values.comment:
Apply a function to all items coming after the current focussed location.

    import ListIs

    HoleyFocusList.only "zeroth"
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ "first", "second" ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.mapAfter String.toUpper
        |> HoleyFocusList.joinParts
    --> ListIs.fromCons "zeroth" [ "FIRST", "SECOND" ]
values.type: (a -> a) -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus a

values.name: mapBefore
values.comment:
Apply a function to all items coming before the current focussed location.

    import ListIs

    HoleyFocusList.only "second"
        |> HoleyFocusList.prepend [ "zeroth", "first" ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.mapBefore String.toUpper
        |> HoleyFocusList.joinParts
    --> ListIs.fromCons "ZEROTH" [ "FIRST", "second" ]
values.type: (a -> a) -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus a

values.name: mapCurrent
values.comment:
If an item is focussed in the `HoleyFocusList`, apply a function to it.

    import ListIs

    HoleyFocusList.only "first"
        |> HoleyFocusList.prepend [ "zeroth" ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ "second", "third" ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.mapCurrent String.toUpper
        |> HoleyFocusList.joinParts
    --> ListIs.fromCons "zeroth" [ "FIRST", "second", "third" ]
values.type: (a -> a) -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList (MaybeIs.CanBe hole_ yesOrNever) a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList (MaybeIs.CanBe mappedHole_ yesOrNever) a

values.name: mapParts
values.comment:
Apply multiple different functions on the parts of a `HoleyFocusList` - what
comes before, what comes after, and the current item if there is one.

    import ListIs

    HoleyFocusList.only "first"
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ "second" ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.nextHole
        |> HoleyFocusList.plug "one-and-a-halfth"
        |> HoleyFocusList.mapParts
            { before = (++) "before: "
            , current = (++) "current: "
            , after = (++) "after: "
            }
        |> HoleyFocusList.joinParts
    --> ListIs.fromCons
    -->     "before: first"
    -->     [ "current: one-and-a-halfth"
    -->     , "after: second"
    -->     ]
values.type: { before : a -> b, current : a -> b, after : a -> b } -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList (MaybeIs.CanBe hole_ yesOrNever) a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList (MaybeIs.CanBe mappedHole_ yesOrNever) b

values.name: next
values.comment:
Move the focus of the `HoleyFocusList` to the next item, if there is one.

```monospace
<đźŤŠ> đźŤ‰ đźŤ‡  ->  đźŤŠ <đźŤ‰> đźŤ‡
```

    HoleyFocusList.only 0
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ 1, 2, 3 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.next
        |> Maybe.map HoleyFocusList.current
    --> Just 1

This also works from within holes:

```monospace
đźŤŠ <> đźŤ‰ đźŤ‡  ->  đźŤŠ <đźŤ‰> đźŤ‡
```

    HoleyFocusList.empty
        |> HoleyFocusList.insertAfter "foo"
        |> HoleyFocusList.next
    --> Just (HoleyFocusList.only "foo")

If there is no `next` item, the result is `Nothing`.

    HoleyFocusList.empty
        |> HoleyFocusList.next
    --> Nothing


    HoleyFocusList.only 0
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ 1, 2, 3 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.last
        |> HoleyFocusList.next
    --> Nothing
values.type: HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus_ a -> Maybe.Maybe (HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList item_ a)

values.name: nextHole
values.comment:
Move the `HoleyFocusList` to the hole right after the current item. A hole is a whole
lot of nothingness, so it's always there.

```monospace
đźŤŤ <đźŤŠ> đźŤ‰  ->  đźŤŤ đźŤŠ <> đźŤ‰
```

    import ListIs

    HoleyFocusList.only "hello"
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ "world" ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.nextHole
        |> HoleyFocusList.plug "holey"
        |> HoleyFocusList.joinParts
    --> ListIs.fromCons "hello" [ "holey", "world" ]
values.type: HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList HoleyFocusList.Item a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList (MaybeIs.CanBe hole_ ()) a

values.name: only
values.comment:
A `HoleyFocusList` with a single focussed item in it, nothing before and after it.

```monospace
<đźŤŠ>
```

    import ListIs

    HoleyFocusList.only "wat"
        |> HoleyFocusList.current
    --> "wat"

    HoleyFocusList.only "wat"
        |> HoleyFocusList.joinParts
    --> ListIs.only "wat"
values.type: a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList item_ a

values.name: plug
values.comment:
Fill in or replace the focussed thing in the `HoleyFocusList`.

```monospace
đźŤ“ <?> đźŤ‰  ->  đźŤ“ <đźŤ’> đźŤ‰
```

    import ListIs

    HoleyFocusList.empty
        |> HoleyFocusList.insertBefore "đźŤ“" -- "đźŤ“" <>
        |> HoleyFocusList.insertAfter "đźŤ‰"  -- "đźŤ“" <> "đźŤ‰"
        |> HoleyFocusList.plug "đźŤ’"         -- "đźŤ“" <"đźŤ’"> "đźŤ‰"
        |> HoleyFocusList.joinParts
    --> ListIs.fromCons "đźŤ“" [ "đźŤ’", "đźŤ‰" ]
values.type: a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList holeOrItem_ a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList item_ a

values.name: prepend
values.comment:
Put items to the beginning of the `HoleyFocusList`. Before anything else.

```monospace
đźŤ’đźŤ‹
 \â†“ đźŤŤ đźŤ“ <đźŤŠ> đźŤ‰
```

    import ListIs

    HoleyFocusList.only 1
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ 2, 3, 4 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.last
        |> HoleyFocusList.prepend [ 5, 6, 7 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.joinParts
    --> ListIs.fromCons 5 [ 6, 7, 1, 2, 3, 4 ]
values.type: List.List a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus a

values.name: previous
values.comment:
Move the focus of the `HoleyFocusList` to the previous item, if there is one.

```monospace
đźŤŠ <đźŤ‰> đźŤ‡  ->  <đźŤŠ> đźŤ‰ đźŤ‡
```

    HoleyFocusList.empty |> HoleyFocusList.previous
    --> Nothing

    HoleyFocusList.only "hello"
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ "holey", "world" ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.last
        |> HoleyFocusList.previous
        |> Maybe.map HoleyFocusList.current
    --> Just "holey"

This also works from within holes:

```monospace
đźŤŠ đźŤ‰ <> đźŤ‡  ->  đźŤŠ <đźŤ‰> đźŤ‡
```

    HoleyFocusList.empty
        |> HoleyFocusList.insertBefore "foo"
        |> HoleyFocusList.previous
    --> Just (HoleyFocusList.only "foo")
values.type: HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus_ a -> Maybe.Maybe (HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList item_ a)

values.name: previousHole
values.comment:
Move the `HoleyFocusList` to the hole right before the current item. Feel free to plug
that hole right up!

```monospace
đźŤŤ <đźŤŠ> đźŤ‰  ->  đźŤŤ <> đźŤŠ đźŤ‰
```

    import ListIs

    HoleyFocusList.only "world"
        |> HoleyFocusList.previousHole
        |> HoleyFocusList.plug "hello"
        |> HoleyFocusList.joinParts
    --> ListIs.fromCons "hello" [ "world" ]
values.type: HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList HoleyFocusList.Item a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList (MaybeIs.CanBe hole_ ()) a

values.name: remove
values.comment:
Punch a hole into the `HoleyFocusList` by removing the focussed thing.

```monospace
đźŤ“ <?> đźŤ‰  ->  đźŤ“ <> đźŤ‰
```

    HoleyFocusList.only "hello"
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ "holey", "world" ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.next
        |> Maybe.map HoleyFocusList.remove
        |> Maybe.map HoleyFocusList.toList
    --> Just [ "hello", "world" ]
values.type: HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus_ a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList (MaybeIs.CanBe hole_ ()) a

values.name: squeezeInAfter
values.comment:
Append items directly after the focussed location in the `HoleyFocusList`.

```monospace
        đźŤ’đźŤ‹
đźŤ“ <đźŤŠ> \â†“/ đźŤ‰ đźŤ‡
```

    import ListIs

    HoleyFocusList.only 0
        |> HoleyFocusList.squeezeInAfter [ 4, 5 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.squeezeInAfter [ 1, 2, 3 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.joinParts
    --> ListIs.fromCons 0 [ 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 ]
values.type: List.List a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus a

values.name: squeezeInBefore
values.comment:
Prepend items directly before the focussed location in the `HoleyFocusList`.

```monospace
      đźŤ’đźŤ‹
đźŤŤ đźŤ“ \â†“/ <đźŤŠ> đźŤ‰
```

    import ListIs

    HoleyFocusList.only 0
        |> HoleyFocusList.squeezeInBefore [ -5, -4 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.squeezeInBefore [ -3, -2, -1 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.joinParts
    --> ListIs.fromCons -5 [ -4, -3, -2, -1, 0 ]
values.type: List.List a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus a -> HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus a

values.name: toList
values.comment:
Flattens the `HoleyFocusList` into a list:

    HoleyFocusList.only 456
        |> HoleyFocusList.prepend [ 123 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.append [ 789 ]
        |> HoleyFocusList.toList
    --> [ 123, 456, 789 ]

Only use this if you need a list in the end.
Otherwise, use [`joinParts`](#joinParts) to preserve some information about its length.
values.type: HoleyFocusList.HoleyFocusList focus_ a -> List.List a

name: ListIs

aliases.name: ListIs
aliases.comment:
Describes an empty or non-empty list, making it more convenient than any `Nonempty`.

We can require a [`NotEmpty`](#NotEmpty) for example:

    toNonempty : ListIs NotEmpty a -> Nonempty a

`ListIs` is equivalent to a [`MaybeIs`](MaybeIs) of a non-empty list tuple:

    MaybeIs emptyOrNot ( a, List a )

so we can treat it like a normal [`MaybeIs`](MaybeIs):

    import MaybeIs exposing (MaybeIs(..))

    ListIs.empty
    --> MaybeIs.nothing

    [ ... ]
        |> ListIs.fromList
        |> MaybeIs.map ListIs.head
    --: MaybeIs (CanBe nothingTag_ ()) head_

    toList : ListIs emptyOrNot_ a -> List a
    toList list =
        case list of
            IsJust ( head_, tail_ ) ->
                head_ :: tail_

            IsNothing _ ->
                []

aliases.name: ListWithHeadType
aliases.comment:
Describes an empty or non-empty list where the head type can be different from the tail element type.

Use [`ListIs`](#ListIs) if you have matching head and tail element types.

`ListWithHeadType` is the result of:

  - [`empty`](#empty)
  - [`only`](#only)
  - [`fromCons`](#fromCons)
  - [`fromUnConsed`](#fromUnConsed)
  - [`cons`](#cons)
  - [`mapHead`](#mapHead)
  - [`map2HeadsAndTails`](#map2HeadsAndTails)

This is equivalent to a [`MaybeIs`](MaybeIs) of a `( head, tail )` tuple:

    import MaybeIs exposing (MaybeIs(..))

    ListIs.empty
    --> MaybeIs.nothing

    MaybeIs.map ListIs.head
    --: ListWithHeadType head (CanBe empty_ yesOrNever) tailElement_
    --: -> MaybeIs (CanBe nothingTag_ yesOrNever) head

    tail : ListWithHead head_ tailElement -> List tailElement
    tail listNotEmpty =
        case listNotEmpty of
            IsJust ( _, tailList ) ->
                tailList

            IsNothing _ ->
                []

aliases.name: NotEmpty
aliases.comment:
`NotEmpty` can be used to require a non-empty list as an argument:

    head : ListWithHeadType head NotEmpty tailElement_ -> head

values.name: append
values.comment:
Glue the elements of a `ListIs` to the end of a `ListIs`.

    ListIs.fromCons 1 [ 2 ]
        |> ListIs.append
            (ListIs.fromCons 3 [ 4 ])
    --> ListIs.fromCons 1 [ 2, 3, 4 ]

Prefer this over [`appendNotEmpty`](#appendNotEmpty) if the piped `ListIs` is already known as `NotEmpty`
or if both can be empty.
values.type: ListIs.ListIs appendedEmptyOrNot_ a -> ListIs.ListIs emptyOrNot a -> ListIs.ListIs emptyOrNot a

values.name: appendNotEmpty
values.comment:
Glue the elements of a `ListIs NotEmpty ...` to the end of a `ListIs`.

    ListIs.empty
        |> ListIs.appendNotEmpty
            (ListIs.fromCons 1 [ 2 ])
        |> ListIs.append
            (ListIs.fromCons 3 [ 4, 5 ])
    --> ListIs.fromCons 1 [ 2, 3, 4, 5 ]

Prefer [`append`](#append) if the piped `ListIs` is already known as `NotEmpty`
or if both can be empty.
values.type: ListIs.ListIs ListIs.NotEmpty a -> ListIs.ListIs emptyOrNot_ a -> ListIs.ListIs ListIs.NotEmpty a

values.name: concat
values.comment:
Glue together a bunch of lists.

    ListIs.fromCons
        (ListIs.fromCons 0 [ 1 ])
        [ ListIs.fromCons 10 [ 11 ]
        , ListIs.empty
        , ListIs.fromCons 20 [ 21, 22 ]
        ]
        |> ListIs.concat
    --> ListIs.fromCons 0 [ 1, 10, 11, 20, 21, 22 ]

For this to return a `ListIs notEmpty`, there must be a non-empty first list.
values.type: ListIs.ListWithHeadType (ListIs.ListIs emptyOrNot a) emptyOrNot (ListIs.ListIs tailListsEmptyOrNot_ a) -> ListIs.ListIs emptyOrNot a

values.name: cons
values.comment:
Add an element to the front of a list.

    ListIs.fromCons 2 [ 3 ] |> ListIs.cons 1
    --> ListIs.fromCons 1 [ 2, 3 ]

    ListIs.empty |> ListIs.cons 1
    --> ListIs.only 1
values.type: newHead -> ListIs.ListIs emptyOrNot_ a -> ListIs.ListWithHeadType newHead ListIs.NotEmpty a

values.name: empty
values.comment:
A `ListIs` without elements.

Equivalent to `MaybeIs.nothing`.
values.type: ListIs.ListWithHeadType head_ (MaybeIs.CanBe empty_ ()) tailElement_

values.name: fold
values.comment:
A fold in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/)
where the initial result is the first value in the `ListIs`.

    import LinearDirection exposing (LinearDirection(..))

    ListIs.fromCons 234 [ 345, 543 ]
        |> ListIs.fold FirstToLast max
    --> 543
values.type: LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> (tailElement -> acc -> acc) -> ListIs.ListWithHeadType acc ListIs.NotEmpty tailElement -> acc

values.name: foldFrom
values.comment:
Reduce a List in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    import LinearDirection exposing (LinearDirection(..))

    ListIs.fromCons 'l' [ 'i', 'v', 'e' ]
        |> ListIs.foldFrom "" LastToFirst String.cons
    --> "live"

    ListIs.fromCons 'l' [ 'i', 'v', 'e' ]
        |> ListIs.foldFrom "" FirstToLast String.cons
    --> "evil"
values.type: acc -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> (a -> acc -> acc) -> ListIs.ListIs emptyOrNot_ a -> acc

values.name: fromCons
values.comment: Build a `notEmpty_` from its head and tail.
values.type: head -> List.List tailElement -> ListIs.ListWithHeadType head notEmpty_ tailElement

values.name: fromList
values.comment:
Convert a `List a` to a `ListIs (CanBe emptyTag_ ()) a`.

    [] |> ListIs.fromList
    --> ListIs.empty

    [ "hello", "emptiness" ] |> ListIs.fromList
    --> ListIs.fromCons "hello" [ "emptiness" ]
    --: ListIs (CanBe emptyTag_ ()) String

When constructing from known elements, always prefer

    ListIs.fromCons "hello" [ "emptiness" ]
values.type: List.List a -> ListIs.ListIs (MaybeIs.CanBe emptyTag_ ()) a

values.name: fromUnConsed
values.comment:
Convert a non-empty list tuple `( a, List b )` to a `ListWithHeadType a notEmpty_ b`.

Equivalent to `MaybeIs.just`.
values.type: ( head, List.List tailElement ) -> ListIs.ListWithHeadType head notEmpty_ tailElement

values.name: head
values.comment:
The first value in the `ListIs`.

    ListIs.only 3
        |> ListIs.cons 2
        |> ListIs.head
    --> 2
values.type: ListIs.ListWithHeadType head ListIs.NotEmpty tailElement_ -> head

values.name: length
values.comment:
How many element there are.

    ListIs.only 3
        |> ListIs.cons 2
        |> ListIs.length
    --> 2
values.type: ListIs.ListWithHeadType head_ emptyOrNot_ tailElement_ -> Basics.Int

values.name: map
values.comment:
Apply a function to every element.

    ListIs.fromCons 1 [ 4, 9 ]
        |> ListIs.map negate
    --> ListIs.fromCons -1 [ -4, -9 ]
values.type: (a -> b) -> ListIs.ListIs (MaybeIs.CanBe empty_ yesOrNever) a -> ListIs.ListIs (MaybeIs.CanBe mappedEmpty_ yesOrNever) b

values.name: map2
values.comment:
Combine 2 `ListIs`s with a given function.
If one list is longer, its extra elements are dropped.

    ListIs.map2 (+)
        (ListIs.fromCons 1 [ 2, 3 ])
        (ListIs.fromCons 4 [ 5, 6, 7 ])
    --> ListIs.fromCons 5 [ 7, 9 ]

    ListIs.map2 Tuple.pair
        (ListIs.fromCons 1 [ 2, 3 ])
        ListIs.empty
    --> ListIs.empty

For `ListWithHeadType head ... tailElement` where `head` and `tailElement` have a different type,
there's [`map2HeadsAndTails`](#map2HeadsAndTails).
values.type: (a -> b -> combined) -> ListIs.ListIs (MaybeIs.CanBe aEmpty_ yesOrNever) a -> ListIs.ListIs (MaybeIs.CanBe bEmpty_ yesOrNever) b -> ListIs.ListIs (MaybeIs.CanBe combinedEmpty_ yesOrNever) combined

values.name: map2HeadsAndTails
values.comment:
Combine the head and tail elements of 2 `ListIs`s using given functions.
If one list is longer, its extra elements are dropped.

    ListIs.map2HeadsAndTails Tuple.pair (+)
        (ListIs.fromCons "hey" [ 0, 1 ])
        (ListIs.fromCons "there" [ 1, 6, 7 ])
    --> ListIs.fromCons ( "hey", "there" ) [ 1, 7 ]

    ListIs.map2HeadsAndTails Tuple.pair (+)
        (ListIs.fromCons 1 [ 2, 3 ])
        ListIs.empty
    --> ListIs.empty

For matching `head` and `tailElement` types, there's [`map2`](#map2).
values.type: (aHead -> bHead -> combinedHead) -> (aTailElement -> bTailElement -> combinedTailElement) -> ListIs.ListWithHeadType aHead (MaybeIs.CanBe aEmpty_ yesOrNever) aTailElement -> ListIs.ListWithHeadType bHead (MaybeIs.CanBe bEmpty_ yesOrNever) bTailElement -> ListIs.ListWithHeadType combinedHead (MaybeIs.CanBe combinedEmpty_ yesOrNever) combinedTailElement

values.name: mapHead
values.comment:
Apply a function to the head only.

    ListIs.fromCons 1 [ 4, 9 ]
        |> ListIs.mapHead negate
    --> ListIs.fromCons -1 [ 4, 9 ]
values.type: (head -> mappedHead) -> ListIs.ListWithHeadType head (MaybeIs.CanBe empty_ yesOrNever) tailElement -> ListIs.ListWithHeadType mappedHead (MaybeIs.CanBe mappedEmpty_ yesOrNever) tailElement

values.name: mapTail
values.comment:
Apply a function to every element of its tail.

    ListIs.fromCons 1 [ 4, 9 ]
        |> ListIs.mapTail negate
    --> ListIs.fromCons 1 [ -4, -9 ]
values.type: (tailElement -> mappedTailElement) -> ListIs.ListWithHeadType head (MaybeIs.CanBe empty_ yesOrNever) tailElement -> ListIs.ListWithHeadType head (MaybeIs.CanBe mappedEmpty_ yesOrNever) mappedTailElement

values.name: only
values.comment:
A `ListIs` with just 1 element.

    ListIs.only ":)"
    --> ListIs.empty |> ListIs.cons ":)"
values.type: head -> ListIs.ListWithHeadType head notEmpty_ tailElement_

values.name: tail
values.comment:
Everything after the first value in the `ListIs`.

    ListIs.only 2
        |> ListIs.cons 3
        |> ListIs.append (ListIs.fromCons 1 [ 0 ])
        |> ListIs.tail
    --> [ 2, 1, 0 ]
values.type: ListIs.ListWithHeadType head_ ListIs.NotEmpty tailElement -> List.List tailElement

values.name: toList
values.comment:
Convert the `ListIs` to a `List`.

    ListIs.fromCons 1 [ 7 ]
        |> ListIs.toList
    --> [ 1, 7 ]
values.type: ListIs.ListIs emptyOrNot_ a -> List.List a

values.name: unCons
values.comment:
Convert a `ListWithHeadType a NotEmpty b` to a non-empty list tuple `( a, List b )`.

Equivalent to `MaybeIs.value`.
values.type: ListIs.ListWithHeadType head ListIs.NotEmpty tailElement -> ( head, List.List tailElement )

values.name: when
values.comment:
Keep elements that satisfy the test.

    ListIs.fromCons 1 [ 2, 5, -3, 10 ]
        |> ListIs.when (\x -> x < 5)
    --> ListIs.fromCons 1 [ 2, -3 ]
    --: ListIs (CanBe emptyTag_ ()) number_
values.type: (a -> Basics.Bool) -> ListIs.ListIs emptyOrNot_ a -> ListIs.ListIs (MaybeIs.CanBe emptyTag_ ()) a

values.name: whenJust
values.comment:
Keep all `just` values and drop all `Nothing`s.

    ListIs.fromCons Nothing [ Nothing ]
        |> ListIs.whenJust
    --> ListIs.empty

    ListIs.fromCons (just 1) [ Nothing, just 3 ]
        |> ListIs.whenJust
    --> ListIs.fromCons 1 [ 3 ]
    --: ListIs NotEmpty number

As you can see, if the head is `just` a value, the result is [`NotEmpty`](#NotEmpty).
values.type: ListIs.ListWithHeadType (MaybeIs.MaybeIs emptyOrNot headValue) emptyOrNot (MaybeIs.MaybeIs tailElementEmptyOrNot_ tailElementValue) -> ListIs.ListWithHeadType headValue emptyOrNot tailElementValue

name: MaybeIs
comment:
`Maybe` with the ability to know at the type level whether it exists.

    import MaybeIs exposing (just)

    [ just 1, just 7 ]
        --: List (MaybeIs just_ number_)
        |> List.map MaybeIs.value
    --> [ 1, 7 ]

`MaybeIs` alone will probably not proof any useful,
but we can build cool type-safe data structures with it:

    type alias ListIs emptyOrNot a =
        MaybeIs emptyOrNot ( a, List a )

    type alias NotEmpty =
        MaybeIs.CanBe { empty : () } Never

    type alias Emptiable =
        MaybeIs.CanBe { empty : () } ()

    empty : ListIs Emptiable a_

    cons : ListIs emptyOrNot_ a -> a -> ListIs notEmpty_ a

    head : ListIs NotEmpty a -> a

This is exactly how [`ListIs`](ListIs) is implemented.


## types

@docs CanBe, MaybeIs


## create

@docs just, nothing, fromMaybe


## transform

@docs map, map2, toMaybe, value, andThen, withFallback


## type-level

@docs branchableType

unions.name: CanBe
unions.comment:
`CanBe` is just a cleaner version of this.
It has a simple type tag to make `Never` values distinct:

    type alias NotEmpty =
        CanBe { empty : () } Never

    type alias Item =
        CanBe { hole : () } Never

_Remember to update the tag after renaming an alias._

Now the fun part:

    joinParts :
        HoleyFocusList (CanBe hole_ yesOrNever) a
        -> ListIs (CanBe empty_ yesOrNever) a
    joinParts ... =
        case ( before, focus, after ) of
            ( [], StringEmpty (CanBe yesOrNever), [] ) ->
                IsNothing
                    --â†“ carries over the `yesOrNever` type,
                    --â†“ while allowing a new tag
                    (CanBe yesOrNever)

            ... -> ...

> the type information gets carried over, so
>
>     HoleyFocusList.Item -> ListIs.NotEmpty
>     CanBe hole_ () -> CanBe empty_ ()

Read more in the readme!

unions.name: MaybeIs
unions.comment: Like `Maybe` with type level information about whether it exists. See [`CanBe`](#CanBe).

values.name: andThen
values.comment:
Chain together many computations that may fail.

    maybeString
        |> MaybeIs.andThen parse
        |> MaybeIs.andThen extraValidation

    parse : String -> MaybeIs CanBe Parsed
    extraValidation : Parsed -> MaybeIs CanBe Validated
values.type: (a -> MaybeIs.MaybeIs (MaybeIs.CanBe thenNothingTag yesOrNever) b) -> MaybeIs.MaybeIs (MaybeIs.CanBe nothingTag_ yesOrNever) a -> MaybeIs.MaybeIs (MaybeIs.CanBe thenNothingTag yesOrNever) b

values.name: branchableType
values.comment:
When using `CanBe ... Never` for an argument:

    theShorter :
        ListIs (CanBe { empty : () } Never) a
        -> ListIs emptyOrNot a
        -> ListIs emptyOrNot a
    theShorter aList bList =
        if ListIs.length bList > ListIs.length aList then
            bList

        else
            --â†“ `NotEmpty` but we need `emptyOrNot`
            aList

to make both branches return `emptyOrNot`, we could use

    aList |> ListType.unCons |> ListType.fromUnConsed

also known as: necessary code that nobody will understand.

    aList |> MaybeIs.branchableType

is a bit better.

đź’™ Found a better name? â†’ open an issue.
values.type: MaybeIs.MaybeIs (MaybeIs.CanBe impossibleStateTag_ Basics.Never) a -> MaybeIs.MaybeIs just_ a

values.name: fromMaybe
values.comment: Convert a `Maybe` to a `MaybeIs`.
values.type: Maybe.Maybe a -> MaybeIs.MaybeIs (MaybeIs.CanBe possibleStateTag_ ()) a

values.name: just
values.comment:
A `MaybeIs` that certainly exists.

    MaybeIs.just "you" |> MaybeIs.value
    --> "you"
values.type: value -> MaybeIs.MaybeIs just_ value

values.name: map
values.comment:
Transform the value in the `MaybeIs` using a given function:

    import MaybeIs exposing (just, nothing)

    MaybeIs.map abs (just -3) --> just 3
    MaybeIs.map abs nothing --> nothing
values.type: (a -> b) -> MaybeIs.MaybeIs (MaybeIs.CanBe nothingTag_ yesOrNever) a -> MaybeIs.MaybeIs (MaybeIs.CanBe mappedNothingTag_ yesOrNever) b

values.name: map2
values.comment:
If all the arguments exist, combine them using a given function.

    import MaybeIs exposing (just, nothing)

    MaybeIs.map2 (+) (just 3) (just 4) --> just 7
    MaybeIs.map2 (+) (just 3) nothing --> nothing
    MaybeIs.map2 (+) nothing (just 4) --> nothing
values.type: (a -> b -> combined) -> MaybeIs.MaybeIs (MaybeIs.CanBe aNothingTag_ yesOrNever) a -> MaybeIs.MaybeIs (MaybeIs.CanBe bNothingTag_ yesOrNever) b -> MaybeIs.MaybeIs (MaybeIs.CanBe combinedNothingTag_ yesOrNever) combined

values.name: nothing
values.comment: Nothing here.
values.type: MaybeIs.MaybeIs (MaybeIs.CanBe possibleStateTag_ ()) a_

values.name: toMaybe
values.comment: Convert a `MaybeIs` to a `Maybe`.
values.type: MaybeIs.MaybeIs justOrNothing_ a -> Maybe.Maybe a

values.name: value
values.comment:
Safely extracts the `value` from a `MaybeIs (CanBe nothingTag_ Never) value`.

    import MaybeIs exposing (just, MaybeIs)

    just (just (just "you"))
        |> MaybeIs.value
        |> MaybeIs.value
        |> MaybeIs.value
    --> "you"

    head : MaybeIs (CanBe empty_ Never) ( a, List a ) -> a
    head =
        MaybeIs.value >> Tuple.first

See [`CanBe`](#CanBe) and [`ListIs`](ListIs).
values.type: MaybeIs.MaybeIs (MaybeIs.CanBe impossibleStateTag_ Basics.Never) value -> value

values.name: withFallback
values.comment:
Lazily use a fallback value if the `MaybeIs` is [`nothing`](#nothing).

    import Dict

    Dict.empty
        |> Dict.get "Tom"
        |> MaybeIs.fromMaybe
        |> MaybeIs.withFallback (\() -> "unknown")
    --> "unknown"

Hint: `MaybeIs.withFallback never` is equivalent to `MaybeIs.value`.
values.type: (unitOrNever -> value) -> MaybeIs.MaybeIs (MaybeIs.CanBe stateTag_ unitOrNever) value -> value
